evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: graph.facebook.com/{API_VERSION}/{PHONE_NUMBER_ID}/messages endpoint, messaging_product=whatsapp, template payload shape, HMAC-SHA256 with APP_SECRET for X-Hub-Signature-256, and hub.mode/hub.verify_token/hub.challenge verification all match documented WhatsApp Cloud API pattern. API_VERSION v17.0 is somewhat dated but stated explicitly.
evidence.executability: Code parses; pydantic-settings BaseSettings is imported correctly. However, GET /webhook uses Python params hub_mode/hub_verify_token/hub_challenge — FastAPI will not auto-map these from the actual query string keys hub.mode/hub.verify_token/hub.challenge (dots not valid in Python identifiers, need alias via Query). This is a real bug. Also relies on ngrok in test flow — sandbox-infeasibility pattern matched, caps at 2.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: env vars listed with acquisition, pip install bash block, .env template, FastAPI backend code, React frontend code, curl test commands. Env var names consistent between list and code. Minor: webhook GET param mismatch and ngrok dependency for full test.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Official developers.facebook.com docs cited inline near relevant sections (Graph API, webhook verification), plus FastAPI/Vite/CRA official docs. Some Meta URLs use /documentation/ path which may not resolve, but domain is canonical.

rubric_notes: ngrok present in test instructions caps EXECUTABILITY at 2. Webhook GET query-param alias bug noted but does not change cap. docs_unavailable=true: long-tail handling applied, scored on internal consistency + explicit version, giving CORRECTNESS=4.
